Container.Name: docker-dashboard
Container.Overview:
A self-hosted Docker dashboard for Unraid. Monitor all containers with live CPU/RAM/network stats, historical graphs, log viewer, update checker (supports Docker Hub, GHCR, LSCR and private registries), one-click update with automatic backup and rollback.
All containers are visible and controllable by default. To protect a container from being stopped/updated add the label: com.kronborg.dashboard.protected=true To hide a container from the dashboard add: com.kronborg.dashboard.excluded=true
Container.Requires: Docker socket access (/var/run/docker.sock) is required. The container runs as root to access the Docker socket.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the web interface. Open http://[IP]:[PORT] in your browser.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Docker socket — required to read and control containers. Do not change this path.

Config.Name: Config & Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite database, container event history, stats history and backups. Stored at /mnt/user/appdata/docker-dashboard by default.

Config.Name: Stats Collection Interval
Config.Description: How often (in seconds) CPU/RAM/network stats are collected per container. Lower = more data but higher CPU usage. Recommended: 30.

Config.Name: Basic Auth Username
Config.Description: Optional. Set both username and password to enable HTTP basic auth on the dashboard. Leave empty to disable auth.

Config.Name: Basic Auth Password
Config.Description: Optional. Password for HTTP basic auth. Only active when both username and password are set.

Config.Name: Private Registry URL
Config.Description: Optional. Base URL of your private Docker registry (e.g. registry.example.com). Used for update checking of private images.

Config.Name: Private Registry Token
Config.Description: Optional. Bearer token for authenticating against your private registry when checking for updates.
